task_completion.description: Open the Sygic GPS Navigation application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sygic GPS Navigation app interface, such as the map view or main dashboard, is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Access the main menu of the Sygic app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main navigation drawer or menu panel of the Sygic app is open, displaying options like 'Settings'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Settings menu within the app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Settings screen of the Sygic app is visible, showing various configuration categories.

task_completion.description: Locate the setting related to keeping the screen on or preventing screen lock.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A setting with keywords such as 'Keep screen on', 'Prevent screen lock', 'Display', 'Sleep', or 'Awake' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enable the 'Keep screen on' or 'Prevent screen lock' setting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The toggle switch, checkbox, or radio button associated with the screen-on setting is clearly in the 'ON', 'Enabled', or 'Checked' state.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal emulators, command-line interfaces, or ADB shell windows visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Sygic GPS Navigation app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, branding, and menus match the Sygic app, rather than a text editor modifying XML files or a generic mock app.

integrity.description: The setting was changed within the Sygic app, not the Android system settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The settings menu shown belongs to the Sygic app (often indicated by app-specific headers or navigation styles), not the global Android OS Display settings.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on the real Sygic GPS Navigation Android app (package: com.sygic.aura). The target setting typically contains keywords like 'screen', 'display', 'sleep', 'lock', or 'awake'. The setting must be toggled to an enabled/true state (e.g., switch turned on or checkbox checked) within the app's settings menu.
